1412.name: Rivertop Trail
1412.description: The edge of a wet forest creeps into a grassy field along the edges of a path here, winding its way along the top of a northern riverbank. The glistening water of the Brown River flows past just to the south, but the bank is too steep to descend, leaving east and west as the only possibly directions of travel.

e.to_room_name: Northern Slopes of the River Valley

2694.name: The River of Bruenor's Woe
2694.description: As you follow the river from the north toward the valley to the south, you begin to hear a faint roaring sound, and you note that the river has narrowed and the current become swifter. The grassy slopes to the west lead along the hillside toward the forest.

w.to_room_name: Northern Slopes of the River Valley

3308.name: Northern Slopes of the River Valley
3308.description: Here the River of Bruenor's Woe turns west and begins its drop into the forested valley where it metamorphoses into the Brown River. From the southwest, you hear the roar of falling water and spot small clouds of mist as they dissolve in the air above point where the river vanishes. The banks are too steep to the south for you to enter, but you could enter just east of here. Trees rise to your west, offering another possible path to the river.

3309.name: Bend in the River
3309.description: The river turns here from the north to the west as it prepares to drop into the river basin. The roar of falling water is now clearly audible. The river is too swift and the banks too high for you to do anything but follow the current to the west or try madly to battle it and return north.

3310.name: The Falls
3310.description: You have passed beyond the turning point, and no longer have any choice but to fall with the water into the valley to the west. Although the fall is not long enough to often be deadly, it will certainly be painful. The banks are far too steep to leave the current, and the current is far too powerful for you to overcome.

3311.name: Flowing among the Trees
3311.description: The river here is slow and wide, weary from its rapid journey over the falls to the east. It flows listlessly westward through the trees that border it on both sides. To the west, the forest canopy becomes thicker, but the sky over enter the forest on the north or south.

3312.name: North Bank of the River
3312.description: The trees crowd close to the river along this muddy stretch of riverbank. After tumbling down the falls just upstream, the waters here become calm and tranquil, and you notice a few fish darting about in the depths. East of here, a sharp grade prevents any travel, while to the west the way is blocked by an abundance of hanging vines. You might be able to force your way through them, but the thorns on the vines look terribly long and sharp. A trail leads north up the hill through the trees. A graceful willow tree stands here, its trailing branches swaying gently.

3600.name: Stout Cabin
3600.description: There is no doubt this cabin was built by a man who knows his business and loves working with good wood. All joints snug and precisely fitted, surfaces smooth and rubbed down with oil. A doorway is to the south.

3313.name: Trail to the River
3313.description: The trail runs downhill from the east to the south, bending toward the river and avoiding the silent and dark forest to the north and west. A songbird sings from the heights of a nearby tree, lifting your spirits. You listen for a while, but the bird soon falls silent, leaving you only the memory of its happiness. The dense forest blocks the way west, but you can enter it to the north or follow the trail east or south.

n.to_zone: The Dark Forest - Brown River Valley

3315.name: Shaded Trail
3315.description: The woods here stand in perpetual twilight, and to the west the twilight gives way to night. The trees bordering the trail to the north are too closely spaced and the undergrowth too thick for you to pass, but you can walk down west.

e.to_zone: The Dark Forest - Brown River Valley

2198.name: Southern Slopes of the River Valley
2198.description: As the ochre gulls circle overhead, your eyes are drawn again to the dark forest below you to the north. A thin line of black trunks rising against the dark green canopy marks the narrow break running along the river. The canopy is otherwise solid. A dusty trail leads south toward a tree-dotted prairie stretching to the southern horizon. The slope on which you stand runs uphill from west to east, and to the north are the trees.

n.to_zone: Silver Lake Caverns - The Dark Forest

1463.name: Northern Slopes of the River Valley
1463.description: Here the low grasses follow the river as it winds down into the valley to the southwest. A thin forest borders this strip of grassy slope on the west, also paralleling the river's course. Above the soft gurgling of the water, all is silent, broken only occasionally by the distant cry of a bird. You can walk north or south along the river, move into the forest to the west, or enter the river to the east.

s.to_room_name: Northern Slopes of the River Valley

2204.name: Northern Slopes of the River Valley
2204.description: The land here slants lazily down toward the Brown River to the south. To the north and beyond the river to the south, it climbs up the twin hills that cradle the river. Directly to the east, a dense, dark forest rises from the hillside and hides the river upstream. You can move north along the slope, enter the forest to the east, or plunge into the river to the south.

n.to_room_name: Northern Slopes of the River Valley

2205.name: East of Oldgate
2205.description: You float on the brown, muddy waters of the river as it flows from east to west toward the city of Oldgate. Grassy slopes lead uphill to the north and south. Just upstream to the east, a thick tangle of trees and vines springs up from the banks.

e.to_zone: The Dark Forest - Brown River Valley

n.to_room_name: Northern Slopes of the River Valley

1630.name: Boat Yard
1630.description: Piles of lumber, kegs of nails, coils of rope; all the assorted materials required by a busy shipwright. Though the yard is well filled, it doesn't seem cramped or cluttered, as it is all well organized. A stout cabin is to the north and a trail leads south.

1628.name: The River of Bruenor's Woe
1628.description: The river flows fairly swiftly from north to south as it winds its way toward the river valley. You notice a number of goats grazing on the grassy slopes far to the west, and think you might have seen a bear wandering through the trees.

w.to_room_name: Northern Slopes of the River Valley
